title: BC FLA Separation Agreement – Standard Clauses, Execution & ILA Certificates

WitnessBlock.description: Witness attestation block for a party's signature.

witness_full_name.description: Full legal name of the witness.

witness_signature.description: True when the witness has signed.

ILACertificate.description: Independent Legal Advice certificate appended per party.

party_name.description: Name of the party who received ILA.

lawyer_name.description: Full name of the advising lawyer.

lsbc_member_number.description: Law Society of British Columbia member number.

duration_minutes.description: Duration of the ILA session in minutes.

confirmations.description: Mandatory confirmations the ILA lawyer must attest to.

reviewed_entire_agreement.description: Lawyer reviewed the entire agreement with the client.

client_appeared_to_understand.description: Client appeared to understand the terms and consequences.

free_of_duress_coercion_undue_influence.description: Client appeared free of duress, coercion, or undue influence.

reasonable_opportunity_for_questions.description: Client had a reasonable opportunity to ask questions and have them answered.

client_confirmed_full_disclosure.description: Client confirmed that full financial disclosure had been made.

lawyer_is_independent.description: Lawyer is independent and does not also act for the other spouse.

lawyer_signature.description: True when the ILA lawyer has signed the certificate.

NotaryBlock.description: Optional notarization block, recommended where agreement effects real property transfers.

notary_name.description: Full name of the notary public.

notary_commission_number.description: BC notary commission or appointment number.

commission_expiry.description: Expiry date of the notary's commission.

notary_signature.description: True when the notary has signed and sealed.

releases_and_waivers.description: Mutual releases of all claims arising from the relationship, subject to FLA s. 4 and s. 93 set-aside jurisdiction.

mutual_general_release.description: Text of the mutual general release of claims arising from the relationship, subject to FLA s. 4 (agreement requirements) and s. 93 (set-aside powers).

property.description: Release of all property division claims under FLA Part 5.

support.description: Release of spousal support claims except as provided in this agreement.

parenting.description: Release of parenting claims except as provided; subject to best-interests reservation.

estate_wills_variation.description: Release of estate claims, including acknowledgment of WESA s. 60 wills variation rights being waived for spousal claims.

tort.description: Release of tort claims arising from the relationship.

intentional_infliction.description: Release of claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress arising from the relationship.

finality_acknowledgment.description: Acknowledgment that all releases are intended to be final and binding.

children_reservation.description: Explicit reservation: Nothing in this agreement releases claims relating to children, which remain always subject to the court's best-interests jurisdiction.

wesa_s60_acknowledgment.description: Each party acknowledges awareness of WESA s. 60 wills variation rights and waives spousal claims thereunder.

debt_indemnity.description: Each party indemnifies the other for debts allocated to that party under the property/debt division sections.

tax_exposure_indemnity.description: Indemnity for tax liability arising from assets allocated or transferred under this agreement.

disclosure_breach_indemnity.description: Indemnity for loss arising from breach of the full-disclosure representations.

notice_requirement.description: Indemnified party must give prompt written notice of any third-party claim.

right_to_defend.description: Indemnifying party has the right to assume defence of the claim at its cost.

cooperation.description: Indemnified party must cooperate in the defence.

dispute_resolution.description: Tiered dispute resolution: mediation, arbitration, then court.

mandatory_mediation.description: Mediation as the mandatory first step.

mediator_selection_process.description: Process for selecting a mediator (mutual agreement, then roster selection).

cost_sharing.description: Mediation costs shared equally unless mediator or court orders otherwise.

timeline.description: Mediation to commence within 30 days of written dispute notice.

arbitration.description: Arbitration as second tier if mediation fails.

governing_rules.description: Arbitration conducted under BC Arbitration Act and, where applicable, the Notice to Mediate (Family) Regulation [VERIFY].

arbitrator_selection.description: Single arbitrator selected by agreement or appointed per BC Arbitration Act.

registry.description: Named judicial registry (e.g., Vancouver, Victoria, etc.).

cost_rules.description: Party who initiates frivolous proceedings bears the other party's mediator/arbitrator fees and reasonable legal costs.

governing_law.description: This agreement is governed by the Family Law Act, SBC 2011, c. 25, and the laws of British Columbia.

exclusive_jurisdiction.description: The parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of British Columbia.

limitation_suspension_acknowledgment.description: Acknowledgment that under FLA s. 198(5) [VERIFY], limitation periods are suspended while the parties are engaged in qualifying family dispute resolution.

supersession.description: This agreement constitutes the entire agreement and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, understandings, and negotiations.

no_outside_representations.description: Neither party has relied on any representations, warranties, or promises not expressly set out in this agreement.

clause_text.description: If any provision is found unenforceable or invalid by a court of competent jurisdiction, the remaining provisions continue in full force and effect.

amendments_in_writing.description: Amendments only effective if in writing, signed by both parties, with each party having received ILA on the amendment.

electronic_signatures.description: Electronic signatures are permitted under the BC Electronic Transactions Act, SBC 2001, c. 10 [VERIFY].

counterparts.description: This agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original, and all of which together constitute one agreement.

permitted_methods.description: Permitted delivery methods: registered mail, email with read receipt, courier.

addresses_for_service.description: Each party's address for service, cross-referencing the parties section.

party_1_address.description: Party 1's last known address for service.

party_2_address.description: Party 2's last known address for service.

deemed_receipt_rules.description: Rules for when notice is deemed received.

email.description: Upon receipt of read confirmation or, absent confirmation, 2 business days after sending.

courier.description: Next business day after delivery to the courier.

address_change_obligation.description: Each party must notify the other in writing of any change of address within 10 days.

conditions_precedent.description: Conditions that must be satisfied before the agreement becomes binding, if applicable.

court_approval_if_required.description: Court approval required where a party lacks legal capacity or is an undischarged bankrupt.

property_transfer_completion.description: Completion of all property transfers described in this agreement.

ila_confirmation.description: Each party's ILA certificate must be executed and appended before the agreement takes effect.

financial_disclosure_certified_complete.description: Both parties certify that financial disclosure exchange, including Form F8 equivalents, is complete.

default_and_remedies.description: Default events, cure periods, and enforcement mechanisms.

events_of_default.description: Events constituting default: missed support payment, failure to transfer property or assets by deadline, breach of disclosure representations, failure to maintain required insurance.

cure_period_days.description: Number of days after written notice within which the defaulting party may cure (e.g., 15 days).

acceleration_of_obligations.description: Upon uncured default, the non-defaulting party may declare all remaining obligations immediately due.

fla_filing_for_enforcement.description: The agreement may be filed with the court under FLA s. 44 [VERIFY] for property enforcement or s. 148 [VERIFY] for support enforcement.

fmep_enrollment.description: Support obligations may be enrolled with the BC Family Maintenance Enforcement Program for enforcement.

execution_block.description: Signature, witness, and optional notarization blocks for each party.

signature_location.description: City and province where Party 1 signed.
City and province where Party 2 signed.

ila_certificates.description: Separate ILA certificate appended for each party.

schedules_index.description: Index of schedules forming part of this agreement.

schedule_a.description: Schedule A: Complete inventory of family and excluded assets and debts.

schedule_b.description: Schedule B: Sworn financial statements per spouse (Form F8 equivalent).

schedule_c.description: Schedule C: Detailed parenting time calendar.

schedule_d.description: Schedule D: Life and other insurance policies with beneficiary designations.

schedule_e.description: Schedule E: Transfer forms and conveyancing documents for real property.

schedule_f.description: Schedule F: Pension division agreements and required statutory forms.

schedule_g.description: Schedule G: Spousal Support Advisory Guidelines calculation output used to inform support terms.
